# HAL 9000 — Complete Technical Reference

> A local, cross-platform, multimodal AI agent that sees, hears, thinks, speaks, and acts on your machine.

## Overview

HAL 9000 is an open-source Python application that creates a multimodal AI agent running entirely on your computer. It combines webcam vision, microphone hearing, multi-provider LLM reasoning, text-to-speech voice output, and 40 OS-level tools into a unified agent accessible via a browser-based dashboard at localhost:9000.

## Core Subsystems

### Vision (core/vision.py)
- OpenCV webcam capture with MJPEG streaming
- Base64 frame encoding for LLM vision input
- Configurable frame interval (default 2s)
- HUD overlay in browser: scanlines, corner brackets, REC indicator

### Hearing (core/hearing.py)
- PyAudio microphone recording with Voice Activity Detection (VAD)
- RMS-based speech onset detection (configurable threshold)
- Two STT providers:
  - OpenAI Whisper API (cloud, paid)
  - faster-whisper (local, free, CTranslate2-based)
- Wake word detection: "Hey HAL" two-stage (VAD → Whisper keyword check)
- Whisper hallucination filtering

### Brain (core/brain.py)
- Abstract BaseBrain with provider-specific implementations:
  - OpenAIBrain: GPT-4o with OpenAI function calling
  - AnthropicBrain: Claude with Anthropic tool use
  - GeminiBrain: Gemini with Google function declarations
  - OllamaBrain: Local LLM via Ollama with OpenAI-compatible tool calling
- Thread-safe conversation history with token-aware trimming
- Tool result compression for older messages
- Vision frame attachment only when user asks about vision (keyword detection)

### Voice (core/voice.py)
- Three TTS providers, switchable at runtime:
  - Edge TTS (free, ~0.7s latency)
  - ElevenLabs (paid, ~1.2s latency)
  - XTTS (local voice cloning, ~4.5s latency)
- Browser audio pipeline: synthesize → serve via API → AudioContext → AnalyserNode → waveform

### Tools (core/tools/)
- 40 tools across 8 domain modules:
  - shell.py: run_shell (77 whitelisted commands, blocklist for dangerous ones)
  - apps.py: open/quit/list applications, app_action (automation), open URLs
  - files.py: list/read/write/search/info files
  - system.py: volume, brightness, notifications, clipboard, screenshot (cross-platform)
  - web.py: web_search (DuckDuckGo), fetch_url
  - memory.py: remember, recall, forget, list_memories, save_session
  - delegation.py: open_claude_code, delegate_to_claude_code, background_task, list_tasks, cancel_task, orchestrate, list_agents, check_conflicts
  - artifacts.py: create_artifact, update_artifact
- Decorator-based registration with safety levels (safe, confirm, dangerous)
- Format converters: to_openai_tools(), to_anthropic_tools(), to_gemini_tools()

### Platform Abstraction (core/platform/)
- Auto-detects OS via platform.system()
- PlatformAPI interface with 15 methods
- Implementations: MacPlatform, WindowsPlatform, LinuxPlatform
- All tool modules use platform API — no OS-specific code in tools

### Memory (core/memory_store.py)
- Typed entries: fact, decision, preference, task, session_summary
- Fields: id (uuid4), type, content, timestamp, source, session_id, metadata
- Auto-migration from legacy {fact, timestamp} format
- Thread-safe singleton with file-based persistence (memory/facts.json)

### Task Runner (core/task_runner.py)
- Async background task queue using subprocess.Popen
- Configurable concurrency (default 2), timeout (default 600s)
- Real-time stdout progress, cancellation via process.terminate()
- Task results stored in typed memory on completion

### Orchestrator (core/orchestrator.py)
- Spawns multiple named Claude Code agents on parallel tasks
- File conflict detection when agents modify same files
- Result summarization and memory storage

## MCP Server (hal_mcp_server.py)
- FastMCP server exposing 20 tools to Claude Code/Desktop:
  - hal_see, hal_screenshot, hal_speak, hal_listen, hal_time
  - hal_remember, hal_recall, hal_forget, hal_list_memories
  - hal_save_session, hal_get_context
  - macos_volume, macos_brightness, macos_notify, macos_clipboard
  - macos_apps, macos_wifi, macos_battery
  - hal_web_search, hal_fetch_url
- Register: claude mcp add hal-9000 -- python /path/to/hal_mcp_server.py

## Free Mode
- FREE_MODE=true overrides AI_PROVIDER=ollama, STT_PROVIDER=faster_whisper, TTS_PROVIDER=edge
- Zero API keys required
- Requires Ollama installed with a pulled model (e.g. ollama pull llama3.1)
- faster-whisper uses CTranslate2 with int8 quantization for fast CPU inference

## Configuration (.env)
- FREE_MODE: true/false — one toggle for zero-cost operation
- AI_PROVIDER: openai, anthropic, gemini, ollama
- STT_PROVIDER: whisper_api, faster_whisper
- TTS_PROVIDER: edge, elevenlabs, local
- OLLAMA_MODEL: llama3.1 (default)
- WHISPER_MODEL_SIZE: tiny, base, small, medium
- CONTEXT_MAX_TOKENS: 16000 (conversation history budget)
- TASK_TIMEOUT: 600 (background task timeout seconds)
- MAX_CONCURRENT_TASKS: 2
- MAX_AGENTS: 4

## Web Dashboard
- Flask server on localhost:9000
- Single-page app: templates/index.html
- Features: HAL eye with waveform overlay, webcam HUD, chat window, choice sheet disambiguation, task queue panel, artifact workspace, voice selector, power button
- PWA support via service worker + manifest
- SSE-based real-time updates (status, logs, tasks, agents, artifacts)

## Security
- Command whitelist (77 approved) + blocklist
- AppleScript escaping for all user strings
- App action blocklist (do shell script, system events, keystroke)
- Secret file blocking (read_file refuses .env, credentials, etc.)
- Localhost binding by default
- Input length limits (2000 chars)

## Project Structure
- server.py: Flask web server + API endpoints
- hal9000.py: Engine lifecycle, main loop, browser audio
- hal_mcp_server.py: MCP server for Claude Code
- config.py: Settings + env loading
- core/: brain, vision, hearing, voice, memory_store, task_runner, orchestrator, platform/, tools/, knowledge.py
- templates/index.html: Dashboard UI
- assets/: Images, PWA manifest, service worker
- memory/facts.json: Persistent typed memory
- knowledge/: Local knowledge files + remote URL sources
